title: SwimOutlet non-Amazon scrape checkpoint resume (redacted)

messages.content: Continue the FWM non-Amazon scraping work for swimoutlet_com only. Resume SwimOutlet carefully from the prior products.json page 8 / 1,750-product HTTP 429 boundary. Use public product/review endpoints only, stop on 429/captcha/WAF, collect customer review image rows and catalog model image rows when product pages expose model images and measurements, then write/update the SwimOutlet CSV and summary JSON, update the claim file, and report coverage.

Read the non-Amazon scrape triage plan, SwimOutlet active claim, and scrape_swimoutlet_reviews.py. Found the existing scraper restarted catalog discovery from page 1 and retried 429s. Updated the scraper to add checkpointed products.json discovery, terminal rate-limit handling, sitemap product URL discovery, Okendo store-review feed collection tied to product context, catalog-model row extraction, and Okendo store-review checkpointing.

Ran conservative public endpoint resumes with 3.0 second request delay. products.json checkpointed pages 1-100 with 25,000 unique product records. Page 101 returned HTTP 400, treated as a public endpoint pagination boundary rather than a rate limit. Sitemap discovery found 44,902 product URLs across 21 product sitemaps.

Okendo store-review feed was checkpointed in bounded chunks. The checkpoint reached page 2,090 / 209,000 reviews, with nextUrl still present in the historical review tail. No fresh HTTP 429, captcha, or WAF challenge was observed during the bounded resume. Stopped due to runtime/progress boundary, not due to rate limiting.

Final bounded SwimOutlet output after enforcing product-page coverage: 611 retained rows written, including 597 customer_review_image rows, 14 catalog_model_image rows, and 62 rows with image + product URL + size + measurement. The Okendo store-review checkpoint contains 2,714 raw customer-image rows; 2,117 lacked a SwimOutlet /products/ URL and were filtered out to avoid aggregate/homepage rows. CSV and summary JSON were written under FWM_Data/non-amazon/data/step_1_raw_scraping_data/swimoutlet_com. The active claim was updated with checkpoint counts, no-fresh-rate-limit status, and next action to resume from the Okendo store-review checkpoint page 2,091 only if deeper historical tail is needed.

Verification: python -m py_compile passed for data-pipelines/non-amazon/scripts/step_1_raw_scrape/scrape_swimoutlet_reviews.py. The final retained CSV row count was 611, and a CSV validation check found 0 retained rows without a swimoutlet.com/products URL. The summary and checkpoint agree on 2,090 review pages / 209,000 reviews / 2,714 checkpointed raw customer-image rows before final product-page filtering, with store_feed_complete false and nextUrl present.

next_steps: Do not restart SwimOutlet catalog discovery unless there is a specific reason; use the existing products.json and sitemap checkpoints.
If deeper historical coverage is needed, resume only from the Okendo store-review checkpoint in conservative bounded chunks.
Keep the public-endpoint guardrail: stop immediately on 429, captcha, WAF, auth wall, or suspicious-request behavior.

notes: Exact .env sensitive values replaced when present.
Common API key/JWT/AWS key patterns replaced.
Local usernames, private bucket name, Supabase project URLs, and GitHub username redacted.
